title: ophrys oxyrrhynchos subsp. celiensis
intro: Ophrys oxyrrhynchos subsp. celiensis, also known as the Italian bee orchid, is a sub-species of Ophrys oxyrrhynchos, a type of orchid native to Italy. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ophrys oxyrrhynchos subsp. celiensis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. However, I couldn't find any information on specific herbal preparations or commercial uses of this plant, but it's often used in traditional folk medicine and studied for its potential medicinal properties.
